Add-on Treatment.evidence: Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone
Add-on Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: The add-on treatment is Docetaxel. This is explicitly stated in the title of the article: "Androgen Deprivation Therapy (ADT) Plus Docetaxel Versus ADT Alone...". It is also mentioned in the abstract under "Design, setting, and participants" (page 1, Chunk 4): "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients...". Further confirmation is found in the "Patients and methods" section (page 2, Chunk 4): "Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone."

Adverse Events - N (%) | All-Cause Grade.plan_extraction_plan: Not reported. The PDF does not provide data on the number or percentage of patients experiencing all-cause adverse events of Grade 3 or higher in the treatment arm.
Not reported. The PDF does not provide data on the number or percentage of patients experiencing all-cause adverse events of Grade 3 or higher in the control arm.

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.plan_extraction_plan: Not reported. The PDF only reports treatment-related Grade 5 adverse events (deaths), not general Grade 3 or higher treatment-related adverse events.
Not reported. The PDF does not provide data on the number or percentage of patients experiencing treatment-related adverse events of Grade 3 or higher in the control arm.

Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.evidence: Four treatment-related deaths occurred during the course of chemotherapy in the ADT plus D arm including two neutropenia-related deaths (both in the HVD subgroup).
Adverse Events - N (%) | Treatment-relat.plan_extraction_plan: Four treatment-related deaths occurred during the course of chemotherapy in the ADT plus D arm including two neutropenia-related deaths (both in the HVD subgroup). The total number of patients in the ADT plus D arm is 192 (from Table 1 on page 3). Therefore, 4/192 patients experienced treatment-related Grade 5 adverse events.

Not reported. The PDF mentions 'Four treatment-related deaths occurred during the course of chemotherapy in the ADT plus D arm' but does not report any treatment-related Grade 5 adverse events (deaths) for the ADT alone (control) arm.

COE_RCT_IND_OVERALL_RJ.plan_extraction_plan: The column is not reported in the PDF. The document does not provide a formal 'overall risk judgment of individual criteria of evidence' for the trial itself, nor does it use terminology associated with such an assessment (e.g., GRADE or Cochrane Risk of Bias ratings).

Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.chunk_id: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1::1
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.group_name: Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1
Class of Agent in Treatment Arm 1.plan_extraction_plan: The first experimental arm (Treatment Arm 1) is "ADT plus D". ADT (Androgen Deprivation Therapy) is a hormonal therapy, and Docetaxel (D) is a chemotherapy agent. Therefore, the classes of agents are Hormonal Therapy and Chemotherapy.

Control Arm.evidence: Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients; median follow-up of 7 yr.
Control Arm.plan_extraction_plan: The control arm is referred to as "ADT alone" or simply "ADT" throughout the document. For example, on page 1, in the "Design, setting, and participants" section, it states: "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients; median follow-up of 7 yr." On page 2, in the "Patients and methods" section, it further clarifies: "Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone." Tables 1 and 2 also use "ADT" to denote this arm.

Control Arm - N.evidence: From October 2004 to December 2008, 385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm.
Control Arm - N.plan_extraction_plan: The total number of participants in the control arm (ADT arm) is 193. This is explicitly stated in the "Patients and methods" section on page 2: "From October 2004 to December 2008, 385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm." This is further confirmed in Table 1 on page 3, which lists "ADT (n = 193)".

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.evidence: Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone.
Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Treat.plan_extraction_plan: The "Results" section specifies that 192 patients were in the "ADT plus D arm" (page 3, Chunk 5). As per the trial design, all patients randomized to the ADT plus D arm were intended to receive docetaxel. Therefore, 192 patients (100%) in the treatment arm received docetaxel.

Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.evidence: In the GETUG-AFU15 study, in contrast, the vast majority of patients (79%) in the ADT arm received so-called salvage D within a median time of 18.5 mo (95% CI, 2.9-179)
Docetaxel administration - N (%) | Contr.plan_extraction_plan: The "Results" section states: "In the GETUG-AFU15 study, in contrast, the vast majority of patients (79%) in the ADT arm received so-called salvage D within a median time of 18.5 mo (95% CI, 2.9-179)" (page 5, Chunk 5). The total number of patients in the ADT arm was 193 (page 3, Chunk 5). Calculating 79% of 193 gives approximately 152 patients (0.79 * 193 = 152.47).

Gleason score - N (%) | \u2264 7 | Treat.plan_extraction_plan: Locate "Table 1 – Characteristics of the patients (n = 385)". In the row "Gleason score, n (%)", find the sub-row "<7". Under the column "ADT plus D (n = 192)", extract the value "84 (45)".

Gleason score - N (%) | \u2264 7 | Contr.plan_extraction_plan: Locate "Table 1 – Characteristics of the patients (n = 385)". In the row "Gleason score, n (%)", find the sub-row "<7". Under the column "ADT (n = 193)", extract the value "78 (41)".

Gleason score - N (%) | \u2265 8 | Treat.plan_extraction_plan: Locate "Table 1 – Characteristics of the patients (n = 385)". In the row "Gleason score, n (%)", find the sub-row ">8". Under the column "ADT plus D (n = 192)", extract the value "103 (55)".

Gleason score - N (%) | \u2265 8 | Contr.plan_extraction_plan: Locate "Table 1 – Characteristics of the patients (n = 385)". In the row "Gleason score, n (%)", find the sub-row ">8". Under the column "ADT (n = 193)", extract the value "113 (59)".

Original/Follow Up.evidence: To assess the impact of metastatic burden and to update overall survival (OS) data of the GETUG-AFU15 study.
Original/Follow Up.plan_extraction_plan: The study is a follow-up analysis. In Chunk 4, on page 1, the abstract states: "to update overall survival (OS) data of the GETUG-AFU15 study." Also, in Chunk 4, on page 2, under "Introduction", it mentions "updated survival analyses." Extract "Follow Up".

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Trea.plan_extraction_plan: The median duration of the entire on-treatment therapy (Androgen Deprivation Therapy [ADT] plus Docetaxel [D]) is not reported as a single median value in months. The paper states that patients in the GETUG-AFU15 study's ADT plus D arm received a "median number of eight... cycles of D" (page 5, text, "Discrepancies between the GETUG-AFU15 and the CHAARTED studies cannot be explained by treatment exposure because patients received a median number of eight and six cycles of D, respectively"). While this provides the median duration for the docetaxel component (approximately 8 cycles * 21 days/cycle = 168 days, or about 5.5 months), the ADT component of the treatment is continuous, and a combined median duration for the entire regimen is not provided.

Median On-Treatment Duration (mo) | Cont.plan_extraction_plan: The median duration of on-treatment therapy for the control arm (Androgen Deprivation Therapy [ADT] alone) is not reported. ADT is typically a continuous therapy, and the paper does not provide a specific median duration in months for how long patients in this arm received ADT. While median overall survival (OS), biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS), and radiographic progression-free survival (rPFS) are reported, these are measures of time to an event, not necessarily the duration of on-treatment therapy.

Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.evidence: The bPFS was significantly longer for patients randomized in the ADT plus D arm in the overall population at 22.9 mo (95% CI, 19.5-28.4) versus 12.9 mo (95% CI, 11.9–17.7)
Median PFS (mo) | Overall | Treatment.plan_extraction_plan: The median biochemical progression-free survival (bPFS) for patients in the ADT plus D arm (treatment arm) in the overall population is reported as 22.9 months.

The table below Figure 1 shows that 32 patients were still at risk at 8 years for the ADT plus D arm, out of an initial 192 patients. The rate is (32 / 192) * 100 = 16.67%.

The primary and secondary endpoints listed in the abstract and methods section (page 1 and 2) do not include quality of life.

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.evidence: In patients with metastases at diagnosis, median OS was 11.1 mo longer in the ADT plus D arm compared with the ADT arm, without a statistically significant difference, at 52.6 mo (95% CI, 43.3-66.8) versus 41.5 mo (95% CI, 36.3–54.5) (HR: 0.93 [95% CI, 0.69-1.25]; p = 0.6).
Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | S.plan_extraction_plan: The results are reported for patients with synchronous metastases. This is indicated in the text on page 4, section 3.1 "Overall survival", which states: "In patients with metastases at diagnosis, median OS was 11.1 mo longer in the ADT plus D arm compared with the ADT arm, without a statistically significant difference, at 52.6 mo (95% CI, 43.3-66.8) versus 41.5 mo (95% CI, 36.3–54.5) (HR: 0.93 [95% CI, 0.69-1.25]; p = 0.6)." Table 1 on page 3 also includes a row for "Metastases at diagnosis, n (%)".

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.evidence: For patients with metastases after failure of local treatment, median OS was not reached (95% CI, 69.5–NR) in the ADT plus D arm and was 69.8 mo (95% CI, 62.2-NR) in the ADT arm (HR: 0.83 [95% CI, 0.47-1.47]; p = 0.5) (Supplementary Table 2).
Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | M.plan_extraction_plan: The results are reported for patients with metachronous disease. This is indicated in the text on page 4, section 3.1 "Overall survival", which states: "For patients with metastases after failure of local treatment, median OS was not reached (95% CI, 69.5–NR) in the ADT plus D arm and was 69.8 mo (95% CI, 62.2-NR) in the ADT arm (HR: 0.83 [95% CI, 0.47-1.47]; p = 0.5) (Supplementary Table 2)." Table 1 on page 3 also includes a row for "Metastases after treatment for local disease, n (%)".

Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.evidence: Median OS in ADT plus D and ADT arms, respectively, was for HVD patients: 39.8 mo (95% CI, 28.0–53.4) versus 35.1 mo (95% CI, 29.9–43.6) (HR: 0.78 [95% CI, 0.56–1.09]; p = 0.14)
Reporting by prognostic groups - Y/N | H.plan_extraction_plan: The results are reported for patients with high volume disease (HVD). This is indicated in the Abstract on page 1, which states: "Median OS in ADT plus D and ADT arms, respectively, was for HVD patients: 39.8 mo (95% CI, 28.0–53.4) versus 35.1 mo (95% CI, 29.9–43.6) (HR: 0.78 [95% CI, 0.56–1.09]; p = 0.14)". Total Participants - N.evidence: Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients

This value is reported in the "Abstract" section on page 1, stating "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients". It is also confirmed in the "Patients and methods" section on page 2: "From October 2004 to December 2008, 385 patients were enrolled including 193 in the ADT arm and 192 in the ADT plus D arm." Additionally, Table 1 on page 3 is titled "Characteristics of the patients (n = 385)", and Table 2 on page 4 includes "All patients n = 385".

Treatment Arm(s).value: ADT plus D versus ADT alone
Treatment Arm(s).evidence: Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients
Treatment Arm(s).plan_extraction_plan: The treatment arms are "ADT plus D" (Androgen Deprivation Therapy plus Docetaxel) and "ADT alone" (Androgen Deprivation Therapy alone). This information is explicitly stated multiple times. On page 1, in the Abstract, under "Design, setting, and participants": "Randomized phase 3 trial of ADT plus D versus ADT alone in 385 mNCPC patients; median follow-up of 7 yr." On page 2, in the "Introduction" section: "The GETUG-AFU15 trial... assessed the benefits of the addition of D to ADT in patients with mNCPC..." On page 2, in the "Patients and methods" section: "Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone."

Treatment Class.evidence: Impact on overall survival (OS) with chemohormonal therapy versus hormonal therapy
Treatment Class.plan_extraction_plan: The treatment classes for the two arms of the trial are "Hormonal therapy" and "Chemohormonal therapy". This classification is explicitly used in the discussion section when referring to the CHAARTED trial, which had a similar design.

Trial.plan_extraction_plan: The trial name is "GETUG-AFU15". It is explicitly stated in the title of the article on page 1 (text, chunk 4) and in the abstract (text, chunk 4). It is also listed as a study in Table 2 on page 4 (table, chunk 2).

Type of Therapy.value: ADT plus Docetaxel versus ADT alone

Further details are provided in the "Patients and methods" section on page 2, first paragraph: "Eligible patients were randomized in a 1:1 ratio to ADT plus D (75 mg/m² for 21 d, up to nine cycles) or ADT alone. ADT consisted of orchiectomy or luteinizing hormone-releasing hormone agonists, alone or combined with nonsteroidal androgen receptor inhibitors."
